_comment: sovereign-os trinity dashboard (R494, R290-R299+ E5). Visualizes the operator-§1g Trinity 3-tier inference stack — pulse (8081, fast tier) / logic-engine (8082, reasoning tier) / oracle-core (8083, deep tier). Sourced from sovereign_os_inference_* metrics (Layer B). Closes the trinity dashboard:FUTURE waiver and registers trinity at 5 §1g surfaces (core/cli/api/service/dashboard). Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model.

panels.title: logic-engine routes (total)
panels.description: Trinity tier-2 (reasoning, mid-class). 127.0.0.1:8082 backend. Operator-routed for tasks requiring chain-of-thought.

panels.title: oracle-core routes (total)
panels.description: Trinity tier-3 (deep, frontier-class). 127.0.0.1:8083 backend. Operator-reserved for the hardest tasks.

panels.title: last route age (s)
panels.description: Operator-§1g freshness signal — staleness of Trinity routing decisions.

panels.title: Trinity route rate per tier (5m)
panels.description: Per-tier inference route rate. pulse / logic_engine / oracle_core — operator-revealed-preference for routing distribution across the 3-tier ladder.

panels.title: Trinity route per task-type (5m)
panels.description: Per-task-type route rate — operator's revealed task distribution.

panels.title: Trinity backend start results
panels.description: Per-tier success/skip/fail count. Operator-§1g lifecycle — if a tier's start_total has fail>0, that tier is silently degraded.

panels.title: Trinity backend PIDs (live)
panels.description: PID per tier — non-existent rows indicate the tier is down.

panels.title: Router class distribution (5m)
panels.description: Operator-§1g routing-class — how requests are bucketed by the router heuristics.

options.content:
**Operator §1g verbatim** (sacrosanct):

> "We do not minimize anything."

The Trinity is the operator-§1g 3-tier local inference stack — three operator-named backends running locally on the workstation, exposed as 127.0.0.1 ports, routed by a single operator-controlled router.

**3-tier Trinity ladder (sacrosanct, R290-R299, E5):**

1. `pulse` (8081) — fast tier (7B-class). Operator-named: 'pulse'. Default first-touch; the operator-discoverable always-on backend. The tier the router falls back to when class detection is ambiguous.
2. `logic-engine` (8082) — reasoning tier (mid-class). Operator-named: 'logic-engine'. Routed when the request needs chain-of-thought, code generation, or multi-step reasoning.
3. `oracle-core` (8083) — deep tier (frontier-class). Operator-named: 'oracle-core'. Reserved for the hardest tasks — long-context analysis, deep code review, planning.

**Routing:** `scripts/inference/router.py` exposes the operator-§1g routing decision via Layer-B metrics. Tier choice = revealed preference per task-class.

**Lifecycle:**

```
scripts/inference/start-pulse.sh         # 7B-class
scripts/inference/start-logic-engine.sh  # reasoning
scripts/inference/start-oracle-core.sh   # frontier
```

Each `start-*.sh` is operator-idempotent (re-run safely) and emits backend_start_total{tier,result=success|skip|fail}.

**CLI:**

```
sovereign-osctl trinity status          # 3-tier health + routes
sovereign-osctl trinity start <tier>    # operator-controlled lifecycle
sovereign-osctl trinity stop <tier>
sovereign-osctl trinity route --tier <name> --task <type>   # explicit
```

**Metrics:** 

- `sovereign_os_inference_route_total{tier,task_type,class}` (Layer B)
- `sovereign_os_inference_backend_start_total{tier,result}`
- `sovereign_os_inference_backend_pid{tier}`
- `sovereign_os_inference_router_last_route_timestamp`
- `sovereign_os_inference_router_task_type_total{task_type}`
- `sovereign_os_inference_router_class_total{class}`

**Source:** R290-R299 (E5) shipped the 3-tier Trinity stack + router. R494 (E5+) ships THIS dashboard + extends trinity surface-map registration to 5 surfaces (core/cli/api/service/dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os trinity
